free_text: Measurements of neutron total and capture cross
sections of 139La and evaluation of resonance
parameters
Energy resolution of the pulsed neutron was given.
ANNRI(BL04) beam line of MLF at J-PARC
Spallation reaction in a mercury target
irradiated with 3-GeV protons (800 kW) .
The proton beams were supplied at a repetition rate of
25 Hz and in the "double-bunch mode", in which the
pulsed protons consist of two bunches, each with a
width of 100 ns, at intervals of 600 ns.
Produced neutrons were slowed down in a supercritical
para hydrogen moderator.
Disk chopper with a semicircular shape was used to cut
the low-energy tail of neutron pulse.
For the Capture Experiment:
-Size: 330 mm (diam.) and 203 mm in thickness
-Angle: 90 deg with respect to the neutron beam line
-Flight length: 27.9 m
-DAQ: CAEN V1720 Digitizer (12bit, 250MHz)
For the Total Experiment:
-Crystal: 6Li(>95%) loaded glass (GS-20:Saint Gobain)
-Size: 50 mm x 50 mm and a thickness of 1 mm
-Flight length: 28.7 m
-PMT: HAMAMATSU H7195
-DAQ: CAEN V1720 (12bit, 250MHz)
For background estimation, same size 7Li(>99.99%)
loaded glass detector (GS-30:Saint Gobain) was used.
Metallic natural La samples
For the Capture Experiment:
-Size: 40 mm x 40 mm and a thickness of 10 mm
-Areal density: 0.02698 +- 0.00013 atoms/barn
-Impurity: 17 ppm of Sm, 32 ppm of Gd, 110 ppm of Er
For the Total Experiment:
-Size: 10 mm in diam. and 0.175 mm in thickness
-Areal density: 0.0004669 +- 0.0000093 atoms/barn
-Impurity: Only the upper limit values were given.
By A. Kimura
Measurements with the carbon sample and
"Blank (no sample) " were used for background
estimation. The relative neutron spectrum was
estimated using the boron sample. The weighting
function was normalized by comparing the experimental
results of the Au sample with JENDL-5, 98.649 b.
The dead-time correction was applied with the
extended dead-time model. Correction factors for
multiple scattering were deduced using the Monte-Carlo
simulation code PHITS. Self-shielding correction was
not applied. The influence was evaluated in the
resonance analysis in 23613.006.

Uncertainty due to normalization

Data in Fig. 9 were obtained from AUTHOR.
Frame-overlap and constant backgrounds were estimated
by fitting a single exponential plus constant
function to the TOF spectrum.
Backgrounds due to gamma-rays were deduced using
the result of the 7Li-glass detector.
The measurements of Au samples were also carried out
to determine the flight length and the initial delay.
The dead-time correction was applied using the
extended dead-time model.

Unc. due to neutron flux
Unc. due to sample thickness
Data in Fig. 4 were obtained from AUTHOR.

Frame-overlap and constant backgrounds were estimated
by fitting a single exponential plus constant
function to the TOF spectrum.
Backgrounds due to gamma-rays were deduced using
the result of the 7Li-glass detector.
The measurements of Au samples were also carried out
to determine the flight length and the initial delay.
The dead-time correction was applied using the

Data were obtained from AUTHOR.
Resonance parameters and spin-dependent
scattering radii were extracted from a combined fit
of the capture cross-sections and the transmission
ratios using the REFIT.
- The ANNRI beamline resolution function was taken
from the work of Kino. See REL-REF.
- The initial resonance parametrization data were
taken from JENDL-5.
- The fitting regions were 0.5 - 74 eV for the
capture CS and from 0.5 - 1500 eV for the transmission.
- The spin and angular momentum of the resonances
were fixed to the values in JENDL-5 except for the
72-eV resonance. The spin of the 72-eV resonance was
used to be 3 instead of 4 recorded in JENDL-5
The uncertainty of resonance energy was
deduced from the uncertainty of the fitting result by
REFIT, the flight length, and the initial time delay.
The other uncertainties are the ones provided by REFIT.
